items.title: Zambry rejects claims Malaysia's Israel policy is anti-Semitic
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said Malaysia's refusal to recognise Israel was not linked to anti-Semitism, rejecting such claims. Speaking after a public lecture by Columbia University professor Joseph A. Massad titled 'Muslim Identity in a Globalised World' today, Zambry said he concurred with Massad's rejection of claims that criticism of Israel amounted to anti-Semitism. He said the criticism against Malaysia was linked to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's stance on Israel, adding that Putrajaya could not accept Israel's actions, which Massad had described as a "genocidal state".

items.title: Highway to the animal kingdom: WCE adopts Taiping Zoo's resident gaur
items.summary: TAIPING, July 23 — West Coast Expressway Sdn Bhd (WCE) has contributed RM10,000 to the Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) in support of wildlife conservation efforts through the Zoo Taiping & Night Safari (ZTNS) Animal Adoption Scheme. WCE chief executive officer Lyndon Alfred Felix said the contribution, made under the company’s corporate social responsibility (CSR) programme, would help cover the care costs of a 12-year-old male ‘seladang’ or Malayan gaur named Bima, which weighs more than 200 kilogrammes. MPT president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan was also present.

items.title: European corporate outlook continues to improve as earnings season gathers steam
items.summary: LONDON: Estimates for European blue-chip companies' earnings have improved, the latest LSEG I/B/E/S data showed on Wednesday, as soaring energy-sector profits are set to help deliver the best growth in more than three years. Companies on Europe's benchmark STOXX 600 index are expected to report earnings growth of 17.3 per cent in the second quarter, according to the data that is based on results from 77 companies and market estimates for those that are yet to report. Although all but one sector — healthcare — are expected to post profit growth, the aggregate rate is seen at a more moderate 7.2 per cent when excluding the energy sector's 122.6 per cent estimated growth rate.

items.title: Zecon awards RM328mil solar contract to subsidiary
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Zecon Bhd's subsidiary Zecon Renewables (Sarawak) Sdn Bhd has awarded a RM328 million engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract to Zecon Renewables Sdn Bhd. Zecon Renewables is Zecon Group's dedicated EPCC and renewable energy outfit. Zecon Renewables will deliver the full EPCC scope for the 100MWac facility.

items.title: Selangor allocates RM40.5mil for Petaling Jaya flood mitigation
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor government has allocated RM40.5 million to execute immediate and medium-term flood mitigation projects across critical hotspots under the Petaling Jaya City Council's (MBPJ) jurisdiction. State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the initiative is a proactive move to address increasingly frequent flash floods caused by climate change, rapid development, and high-intensity rainfall. He said the plan also encompasses 39 projects to fortify flood defences in high-risk areas, including Seksyen 51A, Kampung Cempaka, and Jalan Kuala Selangor (FT54).

items.title: Online child safety: Over 1,500 harmful posts involving children removed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 1,550 offensive online content items involving children have been removed by licensed service providers following enforcement action by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) between Jan 1, 2022 and June 30 this year. "The MCMC also conducts proactive monitoring of harmful content and takes regulatory action under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 (Act 588) and the Online Safety Act 2025 (Act 866)," the ministry said in a written reply to Senator Michael Mujah Lihan in the Dewan Negara today. It said the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), which came into force on Jan 1, requires licensed application service providers and licensed content application service providers to ensure their platforms are safer, including by addressing child sexual abuse material.

items.title: Court jails former bank analyst over fake investment adviser claims
items.summary: The Securities Commission of Malaysia (SC) in a statement said that Judge Hamidah Mohamed Deril meted out the sentence against Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin after finding that the accused had failed to raise a reasonable doubt in the prosecution’s case. According to SC, Mohd Arif Fadzlee was originally charged on Jan 23, 2024, with two charges for offences under Section 59(1) and 362(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 (CMSA). “Mohd Arif Fadzlee claimed trial to both charges and was released on bail of RM60,000.

items.title: More than 1,500 offensive content items involving children taken down since 2022
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — A total of 1,550 offensive content items involving children have been removed by licensed service providers following firm action by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) from Jan 1, 2022 to June 30 this year. “The MCMC also conducts proactive monitoring of harmful content and takes regulatory action under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 [Act 588] and the Online Safety Act 2025 [Act 866],” said the ministry in a written reply to a question from Senator Michael Mujah Lihan in the Dewan Negara today. On the legal aspect, the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), which came into force on Jan 1 this year, requires licensed application service providers and licensed content application service providers to ensure their platforms are safer, including from child sexual abuse material, it said.

items.title: CZBUCG held multiple meetings with Penang govt before tunnel project tender, MACC witness says in court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The Sessions Court today heard that four to five meetings were held between Beijing Urban Construction Group (CZBUCG) and the Penang government regarding the state’s major roads and undersea tunnel project before the open tender process began. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Senior Superintendent Zulhilmi Ramli, 34, testified that the meetings were attended by CZBUCG representative Datuk Lee Chee Hoe and a representative from the Penang State Economic Planning Unit (UPEN), identified as Azlan. The 38th prosecution witness made the statement while being cross-examined by defence counsel Mohd Haijan Omar during the corruption trial of former Penang Chief Minister Lim Guan Eng over the RM6.3 billion major roads and undersea tunnel project.

items.title: BN to unveil Negri Sembilan election manifesto on Friday
items.summary: REMBAU: Barisan Nasional will unveil its manifesto for the Negri Sembilan election tomorrow night, with a focus on continuing development initiatives that were left unfinished. BN deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the manifesto would build on development plans that were stalled after the coalition lost power in the 2018 General Election. Also present was BN's Chembong candidate, Datuk Zaifulbahri Idris.

items.title: Attempt to pressure Malaysia on Israel policy exposes US selective moralism, double standards
items.summary: LETTER: The recent attempt by eight members of the United States Congress to pressure Malaysia into reversing its policy on Israeli nationals is an unacceptable act of political intimidation. The lawmakers described Malaysia's immigration policy as "antisemitic" and accused the Malaysian government of "hunting down and removing Jews". Malaysia is not conducting a campaign against Jewish people.

items.title: 'Johor aura': BN relies on southern success to convince Negri Sembilan voters
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Barisan Nasional (BN) is banking on the "Johor aura" to win over voters in the Negri Sembilan election, Wanita Umno chief Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad said. She said BN believed its track record in Johor would help convince voters to support the coalition's candidates. Noraini said feedback from Wanita Umno's grassroots machinery indicated growing support for BN candidates after several days of campaigning.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC records statements from 10 witnesses in KWAP eFishery probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recorded statements from 10 witnesses, comprising senior officials from the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) and the Finance Ministry, as part of its investigation into KWAP's RM200 million investment in Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the commission had established a special investigation team led by the Investigation Division senior director, with officers from its Investigation, Legal and Prosecution, and Governance Investigation divisions. The investigation was officially launched on July 17, while investigators entered KWAP's premises on July 20 to begin gathering evidence.

items.title: Police bust love scam syndicate preying Japanese victims, arrest 7 foreigners
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Police have busted an online love scam syndicate preying on Japanese victims, following a raid on a shop lot in the Kristal Light Industrial Park here last Sunday that led to the arrest of seven Chinese nationals. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the raid, which was conducted at 1.40pm on July 20, was based on intelligence that the premises were being used as a call centre for the online scam syndicate. He said the syndicate is believed to have begun operations since mid-June, using the LINE and Langmate apps to create fake identities to deceive victims.

items.title: Tabung Haji RCI report needs cabinet nod before Parliament tabling
items.summary: REMBAU: The release of the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Tabung Haji to the public is subject to cabinet approval before it can be tabled in Parliament. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said any paper to be brought to Parliament must first be considered and approved by the cabinet. He was responding to questions on whether the Tabung Haji RCI report would be made public.

items.title: Fallen construction structure disrupts KTM Komuter, ETS services at Batang Benar
items.summary: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) said the structure struck the overhead line system between Batang Benar and Labu stations, disrupting the power supply and affecting train operations. "There are currently no KTM Komuter services operating between Bangi and Seremban stations. To assist affected commuters, KTMB has deployed three shuttle buses operating between Seremban, Tiroi, Batang Benar, Labu and Bangi stations.

items.title: Singapore woman gets back valuables after leaving bag on bus
items.summary: In a Facebook post, SBS Transit said the passenger, identified only as K, realised she had left her bag on the bus only after alighting from Service 853 at a bus stop in Ang Mo Kio. "Although SBS Transit does not operate Service 853, Senior Assistant Interchange Supervisor Nithyananthan Palne (Nathan) immediately sprang into action. "He contacted Tower Transit Singapore to try to trace the missing bag," it said.

items.title: No one should be shielded if Tabung Haji RCI finds wrongdoing, says Asyraf
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has called for no one to be shielded if the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Tabung Haji finds evidence of misconduct, corruption or abuse of power. Commenting on Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's statement that the RCI report would be made public soon, Asyraf said Malaysians wanted more than just disclosure – they wanted firm action against those found guilty. He said the release of the RCI report would only be meaningful if followed by legal action against those found responsible, regardless of their position or influence.

items.title: Man claims trial to 12 counts of armed robbery, cheating
items.summary: KLANG: A man pleaded not guilty at the Sessions and magistrate's courts here today to 12 charges of armed robbery, theft and cheating involving jewellery and cash worth more than RM90,000 between 2022 and this year. B. Murugan, 52, entered his plea after the charges were read out before Sessions Court Judge Mujib Saroji and magistrates Siti Zubaidah Mamat, A. Karthiyayini and Nor Azilah Mat. At the Sessions Court, Murugan was charged with two counts of armed robbery involving two 74-year-old women in Taman Sri Sentosa and Bandar Sultan Sulaiman on Jan 3, 2024 and Feb 10 this year.

items.title: Teenagers plead guilty to possessing stolen motorcycles
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Two 15-year-old boys pleaded guilty in the magistrate's court here today to separate charges of possessing stolen motorcycles. They were charged under Section 411 of the Penal Code with possessing two stolen Honda C100 motorcycles belonging to two individuals at the Surau Taman Mutiara compound here at 11pm on July 14 and 15. The offence carries a jail term of between six months and five years, as well as a fine, upon conviction.

items.title: Batang Benar construction incident disrupts KTM Komuter, ETS services in N. Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — KTM Komuter Klang Valley and ETS services were disrupted today after a construction incident in Batang Benar damaged the railway’s overhead electric supply system, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said. KTMB said the incident involved bridge construction works by a contractor appointed by the Public Works Department (JKR) for a wayleave project. KTMB said three shuttle bus services have also been provided from Seremban, Tiroi, Batang Benar, Labu and Bangi stations to assist passengers in continuing their journeys.

items.title: 473 prison prospects undergo TVET training in construction since 2024
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 473 prison prospects underwent Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) in the construction sector from 2024 to June this year, equipping them with skills to facilitate their reintegration into society upon release. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said of the total, 184 prospects participated in the programme in 2024, followed by 234 in 2025, with another 55 prospects participating up to June this year. "The training programme is supported by three main certification bodies, namely the Malaysian Skills Certificate (SKM) from the Department of Skills Development under the Human Resources Ministry, the Construction Skills Competency Certificate from the Construction Industry Development Board (CIDB), and the Modular Certificate through industry collaboration.

items.title: Civil servants must remain politically neutral during elections - PSD
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Civil servants have been reminded to remain politically neutral and refrain from siding with any party, particularly during election periods, to preserve the integrity and professionalism of the public service. Public Service Director-General Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said civil servants must continue to uphold the core principles of the public service and remain loyal to the government of the day while carrying out their duties impartially. Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the Public Service Department (PSD) has not, to date, received any reports of civil servants campaigning for political parties or taking leave to participate in campaign activities during the 16th Negri Sembilan state election.

items.title: TH properties earn sustainability accolades for enhanced asset management
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH), Malaysia's Islamic pilgrims' fund, has received two accolades for its flagship properties, Menara TH Tun Razak and Menara Bank Islam, reflecting its continued focus on sustainable asset management, operational efficiency and long-term value creation. Menara TH Tun Razak won the Non-Strata Office category, while Menara Bank Islam was recognised in the Single-Owned Office in a Mixed Strata Development category at The Edge Malaysia Best Managed & Sustainable Property Awards (BMSPA) 2026. Both properties are managed by TH Properties Sdn Bhd, a wholly owned subsidiary of TH.

items.title: [UPDATE] 'Not a new stance': Malaysia reaffirms on non-recognition of Israel to US
items.summary: REMBAU: REMBAU: Malaysia has officially reaffirmed its longstanding policy of not recognising Israel to the United States Department of State. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Malaysia's ambassador to the United States, Tan Sri Muhammad Shahrul Ikram Yaakob, had been contacted by the US Department of State to seek clarification on the matter. Mohamad said the Department of State had acknowledged and respected Malaysia's long-standing policy of not recognising Israel.

items.title: AirBorneo warns passengers against buying tickets from unofficial third-party websites
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) has warned passengers against purchasing flight tickets from unauthorised third-party websites, citing risks including inflated prices, hidden charges and difficulties with refunds or rebooking. AirBorneo said passengers who purchase such tickets may face issues after booking, including delayed or failed booking confirmations, additional administrative charges and problems obtaining refunds or assistance during flight disruptions. AirBorneo said booking directly through its official channels would provide passengers with accurate and updated fares without hidden mark-ups or undisclosed service fees, immediate booking confirmation and the ability to reselect seats without additional costs.

items.title: MoE to increase funding for Asean Islamic science camp
items.summary: NILAI: The Education Ministry is prepared to allocate more funding for next year's Asean Islamic Science Camp (AISC) to expand the programme and allow greater student participation, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said. She said the initiative reflects the ministry's commitment to nurturing young talent through international programmes that promote excellence in science, technology and innovation. Fadhlina said strengthening Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) education and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) remains a national priority.

items.title: Touch ‘n Go rolls out Enhanced RFID lane at AKLEH, targets 10 lanes by year-end
items.summary: Marked with a distinctive gold star on the RFID signage, the upgraded RFID lane works with all existing Touch ‘n Go RFID tags and forms part of the company’s roadmap towards Multi-Lane Fast Flow (MLFF) tolling. Touch ‘n Go showcased the Enhanced RFID lane to the media which uses its Malaysian-developed Titan Flow Technology. Enhanced RFID powered by Titan Flow combines RFID, ANPR and AI The Enhanced RFID lane is powered by Touch ‘n Go’s homegrown Titan Flow Technology, which combines dual RFID antennas, Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to verify vehicles using multiple layers of detection.

items.title: Cops bust solar cable theft syndicate, 9 held
items.summary: KUANTAN: Police have busted the "Geng Lakino Garuda" syndicate with the arrest of nine men suspected of involvement in 21 cases of solar cable and telecommunications component theft, involving losses exceeding RM900,000, following a series of raids in Pahang and Selangor. Acting Pahang police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said those arrested comprised four Malaysians, four Indonesians and one Chinese national, aged between 26 and 52, during operations conducted from June 27 to July 17. Azry Akmar said police are still hunting two other suspects believed to be at large.

items.title: No fiscal hoards: MOF redirects RM2 bil diesel subsidy savings into direct public aid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Savings generated from the targeted diesel subsidy mechanism are not being retained solely as a fiscal surplus but are instead being channelled into strengthening targeted assistance programmes, the Finance Ministry (MOF) said. The ministry said the targeted diesel subsidy implemented under the revamped Budi Diesel programme has delivered two key outcomes: ensuring a stable domestic diesel supply while allowing eligible Malaysians to continue purchasing diesel at a subsidised price. On June 22, the government announced the nationwide standardisation of diesel prices and subsidy mechanisms, effective July 1, 2026, allowing eligible Malaysians to purchase subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre through the Budi Diesel programme.

items.title: Sabah Health Dept says action underway over sexual harassment, bullying allegations involving hospital director
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — The Sabah State Health Department (JKNS) said action is being taken in accordance to procedures following allegations of sexual assault and workplace bullying involving a hospital director in the state capital. In a statement today, JKNS said a detailed investigation into the allegations had already been completed by a special committee appointed by the Ministry of Health Malaysia (MOH). “JKNS stresses that the department does not compromise on any form of misconduct, particularly acts involving sexual assault, harassment and workplace bullying.

items.title: Ex-bank analyst jailed three years for illegal investment activities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court has sentenced former bank analyst Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin to three years in prison for unlicensed regulated activities and the unlawful use of the unit trust consultant title. Arif was originally charged by the Securities Commission (SC) on Jan 23, 2024 with two charges for offences under section 59(1) and 362(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 (CMSA). For the first charge under section 59(1), Arif was charged for holding himself out as a representative of CIMB Wealth Advisors Bhd in relation to the regulated activity of dealing in securities, despite not holding a Capital Markets Services Representative's Licence (CMSRL) from the SC nor being a registered person.

items.title: Why New York's Mamdani cannot have Israel's Netanyahu arrested
items.summary: NEW York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said this week that he had determined he lacked the legal authority to have Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu arrested for alleged war crimes should he visit the largest US city, after earlier saying he was considering the move. Independent legal experts told Reuters that Mamdani lacked authority to have Netanyahu arrested because the US generally recognises immunity for heads of state and United Nations representatives, and because Washington is not a member of the International Criminal Court. Here is a look at the legal issues: WHY DOES MAMDANI WANT NETANYAHU ARRESTED?

items.title: MBSB sells all MIDF Amanah shares to Tortoise Works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MBSB Bhd, through its unit Malaysian Industrial Development Finance Bhd (MIDF), has divested all its stake in MIDF Amanah Asset Management Bhd (MAAM) to Tortoise Works Sdn Bhd. Tortoise Works director Shan Kamahl Mohammad said the company is committed to preserving MAAM's legacy while pursuing sustainable growth, strong governance and strong investment outcomes. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Govt steps up mental health intervention for civil servants
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The government is intensifying early intervention efforts to address mental health issues among civil servants after the MyPsych study identified more than 40,000 public officers as being at risk of negative behaviours, including suicide attempts. Public Service Director-General Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said heads of departments have been instructed to work closely with psychologists to provide early intervention for officers identified as being at risk. He said the government is also examining organisational factors, including workplace environment and organisational culture, that may contribute to stress among civil servants, rather than focusing solely on individuals experiencing mental health challenges.

items.title: Rubio says International Criminal Court has 'lunatics, crazies'
items.summary: MANILA: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday there were "lunatics, crazies" in the International Criminal Court and there was no way any US political or military officials would ever go on trial as the country was not a signatory to its treaty. On Wednesday, member states of the International Criminal Court will vote on Friday on whether to fire chief prosecutor Karim Khan for alleged sexual misconduct in a case that has damaged the tribunal's credibility at a time when the US has ramped up its attacks on the court. In another development, two Ghanaian nationals have petitioned the ICC to open a preliminary investigation into attacks against foreign nationals in South Africa, which they said could constitute crimes against humanity.

items.title: Anwar committed to resolving Kampung Sungai Cot Orang Asli land issue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will continue to facilitate efforts to resolve the land dispute involving the Orang Asli community in Kampung Sungai Cot, Maran, Pahang. Political secretary to the Finance Minister Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said Anwar would raise the matter with the Pahang government to help secure a swift, fair and lawful resolution. "The Prime Minister said that the matter would be brought to the attention of the Pahang government, as land administration and ownership fall under the state government's jurisdiction under the Federal Constitution.

items.title: Pakatan leads N. Sembilan forecast, but BN’s trust advantage keeps election race open, says Vodus survey
items.summary: Vodus Research said PH currently leads the statewide popular vote with 42 per cent support, ahead of BN at 35 per cent and PN at seven per cent, while 14 per cent of voters remain undecided or declined to reveal their preference. The research firm also found BN and PH drawing support from different voter groups, with BN leading among Bumiputera voters at 41 per cent, compared with PH at 27 per cent and PN at 25 per cent. Among Chinese voters, PH held a strong lead at 59 per cent, followed by BN at 20 per cent and PN at nine per cent.

items.title: Ex-bank analyst gets jail for posing as unit trust consultant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former bank analyst was sentenced to three years' jail today after the Sessions Court found him guilty of dealing in securities without a licence and unlawfully using the title "Unit Trust Consultant" (UTC). Judge Hamidah Mohamed Deril handed down the sentence after finding that Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin had failed to raise reasonable doubt against the prosecution's case. Arif was convicted under Section 59(1) of the Capital Markets and Services Act (CMSA) for posing as a representative of CIMB Wealth Advisors Bhd and dealing in securities without a licence.

items.title: Kedah police bust ‘love scam’ call centre targeting Japanese, seven foreigners arrested
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 23 — Police have busted an online love scam syndicate preying on Japanese victims, following a raid on a shop lot in the Kristal Light Industrial Park here last Sunday that led to the arrest of seven Chinese nationals. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the raid, which was conducted at 1.40pm on July 20, was based on intelligence that the premises were being used as a call centre for the online scam syndicate. He said the syndicate is believed to have begun operations since mid-June, using the Line and Langmate apps to create fake identities to deceive victims.

items.title: ‘No Bangkok Move, no Dubai Move’: Zahid says BN committed to unity govt until end of Parliament
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 23 — Barisan Nasional (BN) remains committed to the Unity Government until the end of the 15th Parliament, rejecting claims of any attempt to topple the administration led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Deputy Prime Minister and BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the coalition’s commitment is in line with the Yang di-Pertuan Agong’s decree for the formation of the Unity Government after the 2022 General Election and has never wavered. On reports that a BN polling district centre (PDM) in Klawang had ceased operations amidst the Negeri Sembilan state election campaign, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Umno president, said the matter was being addressed by the state party leadership.

items.title: Former e-hailing driver claims trial to 12 charges in Klang over armed robbery, fraud, theft
items.summary: B. Murugan, 52, pleaded not guilty to all 12 charges, which were read to him in Tamil before a Sessions Court judge and three magistrates, according to Kosmo! The charges, heard before Sessions Court Judge Mujib Saroji, were framed under Section 392 of the Penal Code and read together with Section 397, which provides for up to 14 years’ imprisonment and a fine or whipping upon conviction. The cheating charges were brought under Section 417 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum jail term of seven years or a fine.

items.title: Police cripple ‘Geng Vinod’ organised crime group after 11 suspects detained in Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Police have crippled an organised crime group known as “Geng Vinod” believed to be involved in violent crimes including armed robbery, attempted murder and rioting with weapons in Selangor. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Commissioner Datuk M. Kumar said police launched a special operation codenamed “Op Rapido” against the group. “The group has been involved in various violent crimes such as gang robbery, attempted murder, rioting with weapons, causing injury with weapons and mischief,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Road closures on Besraya, NPE from Aug 1 night for highway challenge event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Several selected sections of the Sungai Besi Expressway (BESRAYA) and the New Pantai Expressway (NPE) will be temporarily closed from 11 pm on Aug 1 to 10 am on Aug 2 to facilitate the IJM Allianz Duo Highway Challenge 2026 event. Information regarding alternative routes will be shared through the official BESRAYA and NPE social media platforms. The event is jointly organised by BESRAYA (M) Sdn Bhd and New Pantai Expressway Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of IJM Corporation Berhad, along with Allianz Malaysia Berhad.

items.title: Two-year-old girl killed after being hit by grandfather’s trailer lorry in Gurun
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 23 — A two-year-old girl was killed after she was hit by a trailer lorry driven by her grandfather at Kampung Bukit Kuang, Gurun, near here, this morning. Kedah Police Chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said police received a report on the incident at 11 am, and the victim was pronounced dead at the scene. “At the time of the incident, the 61-year-old man was reversing the unloaded trailer lorry when he struck his two-year-old granddaughter, causing severe injuries that resulted in her death,” he told reporters at the Kedah Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK) here today.

items.title: Sabah assemblyman questions delay in action against hospital director over sexual harassment claims
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — An opposition assemblyman today questioned why a hospital director accused of sexual harassment allegedly remained in office months after internal investigations were conducted, calling for stronger accountability mechanisms in public institutions. Sungai Sibuga assemblyman Lisa Hassan Alban said the alleged incidents took place in July 2025 at a Hospital in Likas, where two internal investigations were carried out. She said the issue went beyond sexual harassment and reflected broader institutional failures in dealing with alleged abuses of power by public officials.

items.title: Five, including teens, plead guilty over Johor restaurant brawl during World Cup screening
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 23 — Five men, including two teenagers, pleaded guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to fighting and causing a disturbance at a restaurant during a live screening of the World Cup final last Monday. The accused, Abdullah Tenis Munisamy, 38; Keevaan Jay Arkesh Rajendran, 28; Muhammad Nafiz, 19; and two teenagers aged 15 and 17, entered the plea after the charge was read before Magistrate Nurzihan Abdul Rahman. They were charged with fighting in a public place at a restaurant in Taman Daya here at 5.40am on July 20.

items.title: Abang Johari: AirBorneo’s first international flight marks new era for Sarawak as regional economic hub
items.summary: SIBU, July 23 — AirBorneo’s inaugural international flight from Kuching to Singapore on Wednesday marked a historic milestone in Sarawak’s aviation development, said Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. The Premier said this during the Sarawak Day 2026 Celebration at Dataran Tun Tuanku Haji Bujang here on Wednesday night. Abang Johari also envisioned AirBorneo helping to promote Sarawak’s unique local products to international travellers, including dabai (Sarawak olive) from Song and Kanowit.

items.title: Umno leaders cool to Hamzah Zainudin’s ‘Perikatan Barisan Nasional’ proposal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Several Umno leaders have expressed reservations over Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s proposal to name the Barisan Nasional (BN)-Perikatan Nasional (PN) cooperation “Perikatan Barisan Nasional”. Instead, he said the BN-PN partnership was merely an understanding to contest the Negeri Sembilan state election together. “Let us work together with mutual understanding, avoid overlapping seats and win the Negeri Sembilan state election together.

items.title: AirBorneo launches first international route with Kuching-Singapore flight
items.summary: KUCHING, July 23 — AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) launched its first international route on Sarawak Day, connecting Kuching and Singapore through Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4. Marking the airline’s entry into international operations, Flight MY363 departed Kuching at 6.50pm and arrived in Singapore at 8.04pm, 21 minutes ahead of schedule, said AirBorneo in a statement yesterday. AirBorneo chairman Datuk Amar Mohamad Abu Bakar Marzuki said the inaugural international route marked a significant milestone for both the airline and Sarawak.

items.title: A good problem to have? TVET boom leaves polytechnic hostels bursting at the seams, Zambry says fixes on the way
items.summary: SEPANG, July 23 — The government will address the issue of insufficient accommodation facilities at several polytechnics by implementing improvement measures, including repairing hostels identified as requiring attention, said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir. He said the accommodation issue emerged following a significant increase in applications to polytechnics and skills institutions as a result of the government’s efforts to encourage more Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) holders to pursue technical and vocational education and training (TVET). Zambry said the increase in student admissions had resulted in accommodation constraints at several polytechnics, particularly institutions with older hostel buildings requiring maintenance.

items.title: Tanjong Sepat to be free of pig farms by October, says Selangor exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The clearance of all pig farms in Tanjong Sepat, Kuala Langat is expected to be completed by October, bringing pig farming operations in the area to an end. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the timeline would allow the remaining piglets in the area to reach maturity at seven to eight months of age before being disposed of, Kosmo! He said about 12,000 pigs remain in Tanjong Sepat, down from around 240,000 before the phased clearance exercise began.

items.title: Terengganu’s tourism boom: 6.5 million visitors bring up to RM1.5b in revenue
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 23 — Terengganu recorded the arrival of 6.5 million tourists, generating revenue between RM1.4 and RM1.5 billion, in the first six months of this year. State Tourism, Culture, Environment, and Climate Change Committee chairman Datuk Razali Idris said that it involves 70 per cent domestic tourists and 30 per cent international tourists holidaying on resort islands and other tourist destinations. Based on the Domestic Tourism Survey 2025, released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), Terengganu received 15.46 million domestic visitors in 2025, an increase from 14.46 million visitors the previous year, representing a growth of 6.9 per cent.

items.title: Chief Secretary steps in after complaints pile up at Universiti Malaya Medical Centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Chief Secretary to the Government (KSN) Tan Sri Datuk Seri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar has directed that immediate measures be taken to address several critical service issues affecting the Universiti Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM). In a Facebook post today, Shamsul Azri said safeguarding public health remains a top priority of the MADANI Government and had called for a meeting this morning to discuss the matter. According to Shamsul Azri, the meeting focused on addressing policy-related issues arising from public complaints, including long waiting times, challenges in the delivery of clinical services, and issues related to skilled manpower and specialist expertise.

items.title: Climate change takes toll as 350km of Selangor coastline erodes, state exco says
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 23 — About 350km of Selangor’s coastline has been identified as affected by erosion due to various factors, including climate change. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said coastal defence structures, including wave barriers, have been installed along about 250km of the affected areas, according to a report published by Kosmo! Izham said this after officiating the review workshop for the Selangor Integrated Coastal Management Plan here today.

items.title: Sabah aims to boost local meat supply threefold by 2030
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — The Sabah government aims to increase the state’s ruminant meat Self-Sufficiency Ratio (SSR) to 25 per cent by 2030, up from the current 7.05 per cent, the Sabah State Legislative Assembly was told today. Sabah Assistant Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Datuk Hendrus Anding said the state government has undertaken various initiatives to achieve the target, including allocating RM24.3 million to upgrade government-owned livestock farms across the state. Elaborating, Hendrus said the upgrading programme is expected to increase large ruminant meat production by 199 per cent, from 126 head this year to 377 head annually by 2030, while breeding stock output is projected to rise by 78 per cent to 752 head a year.

items.title: Malaysia nearing renewable energy target as solar push gathers pace, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: PASIR MAS, July 23 — Malaysia’s renewable energy installed capacity stood at nearly 32 per cent as of June, putting the country firmly on course to achieve its 35 per cent target by 2030, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said this achievement reflects the nation’s steady progress in driving its energy transition agenda under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR). “Energy transition is not an option.

items.title: Seremban’s Terminal 1 earns Grade A rating, meets highest public transport standards, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 23 — Seremban’s Terminal 1 has received a Grade A rating from the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), signifying that it meets the highest standards in management, operations, safety and user facilities. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the recognition was not awarded to every terminal, as facilities must fulfil various criteria including cleanliness levels, operational efficiency, ticketing systems and passenger comfort, Astro Awani reported today. Loke said this after officiating the handover ceremony for the Grade A Terminal Rating to Seremban Terminal 1 today.

items.title: Perak MB urges youths to think critically, uphold ethics as AI transforms society
items.summary: IPOH, July 23 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today urged young generation to think critically, evaluate information responsibly and make ethical decisions as artificial intelligence (AI), automation and the digital economy reshape the future of work and society. Saarani said education must go beyond academic achievement by equipping the younger generation with critical thinking, problem-solving skills and a strong sense of responsibility. He said youths must be provided with the knowledge, training, guidance and opportunities to innovate as they will become future technology developers, policymakers, entrepreneurs and leaders shaping the world.

items.title: ACCCIM survey finds Malaysian businesses still struggling with financing access, alternative channels underutilised
items.summary: Socio-Economic Research Centre (SERC) executive director Lee Heng Guie said 57.8 per cent of respondents in the M-BECS indicated that they sought financing primarily to support working capital and cash flow needs. As alternative financing channels remain underutilised, Lee said more than half of the respondents, or 54.5 per cent, felt that access to financing had become tighter compared with two years ago. From the findings, Lee said high interest or financing costs and lengthy approval processes remained the most significant barriers to obtaining financing, with 44.5 per cent of respondents citing these challenges.
